summary:
The needle has been found and fixed! The issue was that the Go code in `app/main.go` was using the old column name "status" in SQL queries, but migration `002_rename_status.sql` had renamed this column to "order_status". This schema drift caused the application to fail when trying to insert or select data.

**Changes made:**
1. Updated INSERT statement in `createOrder()` function to use `order_status` instead of `status`
2. Updated RETURNING clause in `createOrder()` function to use `order_status` instead of `status`
3. Updated SELECT statement in `getOrder()` function to use `order_status` instead of `status`

The code has been recompiled and verified. The fix ensures the application code matches the database schema defined by the migrations.
